# Port of etcd-raft's lagging_commit.txt.
#
# Tests that after a network blip drops a follower's ack, the leader's NEXT
# MsgApp to that follower carries the new commit index even if no new entries
# need replication — so the follower's commit catches up via heartbeats or
# subsequent appends instead of waiting for the next proposal.
#
# Without this (etcd's canBumpCommit logic, mirrored in Java at
# Raft.stepLeader's MsgAppendResponse handler), a follower's commit would lag
# indefinitely until a new entry arrives.

add-nodes id=1,2,3 voters=1,2,3
----
INFO 1 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 1 became follower at term 0
INFO 2 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 2 became follower at term 0
INFO 3 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 3 became follower at term 0

campaign id=1
----
INFO 1 is starting a new election
INFO 1 became StateCandidate at term 1

stabilize
----
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateCandidate term=1 commit=0
> 2 receiving MsgRequestVote from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 3 receiving MsgRequestVote from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 1 receiving MsgRequestVoteResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgRequestVoteResponse from 3
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=0
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3

# Propose 2 entries on the leader.
propose id=1 data=data1
----
ok

propose id=1 data=data2
----
ok

# Leader produces the broadcast MsgApp; route them into the followers'
# inboxes via the leader's Ready cycle.
process-ready id=1
----
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=1

# Both followers deliver, append, and send MsgAppResp.
deliver-msgs id=2
----
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1

process-ready id=2
----
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1

deliver-msgs id=3
----
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1

process-ready id=3
----
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1

# Now simulate a network blip: ONLY deliver follower 2's response to the
# leader (drop follower 3's). The leader will hit quorum {1, 2} and commit.
deliver-msgs id=1 from=2
----
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2

# Drop 3's pending MsgAppResp.
drop-msgs id=1 from=3
----
> 1 dropping MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 dropping MsgAppendResponse from 3

# Leader processes Ready: it now has commit=4 (no-op + data1 + data2 + 1
# bootstrap = 4 entries) and broadcasts a new MsgApp carrying that commit
# index to BOTH followers (even though 3 hasn't acked the entries).
process-ready id=1
----
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=3

# Status check: leader has commit=4, follower 2 caught up via the latest
# MsgApp, follower 3 still has commit=1 (the old commit from before).
status id=1
----
id=1 state=StateLeader term=1 lead=1 commit=3 lastIndex=3

status id=2
----
id=2 state=StateFollower term=1 lead=1 commit=1 lastIndex=3

status id=3
----
id=3 state=StateFollower term=1 lead=1 commit=1 lastIndex=3

# Deliver 3's pending MsgApp (with the new commit index 4) — it should now
# catch up without needing a new propose.
deliver-msgs id=3
----
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1

process-ready id=3
----
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=3

# Verify 3's commit is now 4.
status id=3
----
id=3 state=StateFollower term=1 lead=1 commit=3 lastIndex=3
